text: When building websites, security is essential. First, use HTTPS to encrypt data between the user and the server, which prevents hackers from stealing sensitive information. Next, implement strong authentication, like passwords or multi-factor login, to verify users' identities. Input validation is also critical: always check and sanitise user data before processing it. This stops attackers from injecting malicious code, such as SQL commands or scripts, into your site. Common vulnerabilities include cross-site scripting (XSS) and SQL injection, where attackers exploit weak input handling. By following these practices, web developers can protect both their applications and their users from harm.
